feed.title: SAP Community - Settlement Management
feed.subtitle: Settlement Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Database table for business volume data is stored for a condition contract
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Below are the questions i need support on if anyone has worked on custom changes in condition contract settlement process.

Q1.What is the database table in which the Business Volume data is stored for a condition contract(The billing documents and quantities).I need to build a custom logic to fetch the data and perform some calculations on it but i am struggling to fine.

Q2.If we create custom fields in a condition contract header, is it possible to use those fields in a pricing routine or the fields absolutelty need to be part of the pricing structure?

Thanks,

entry.title: Custom Accrual condition not being reversed
entry.content:
We are using standard sale rebate contract (0S01) and pricing procedure A10005. We added one custom condition as Accruals (because we did not want to settle this directly with customer) as ZREP which will be a percentage based condition of the business volume. During the partial settlement rebate condition RES1 is being corrected by RES2 which holds the previous settlement value but the custom accrual condition is not being corrected. It calculates the value based on complete business volume without reversing it. Below are few screenshots of config already done:

Any help will be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: WB2_REFERENCE_DOCUMENTS
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a project that requires the implementation of custom logic using the BAdIs WB2R_DOCUMENT_CREATE and WB2_REFERENCE_DOCUMENTS. However, I have been unable to find detailed information or documentation about these specific BAdIs.

Could anyone provide insights or share documentation on the following points?

  1. WB2R_DOCUMENT_CREATE:

    • Purpose and typical use cases
    • Implementation examples
    • Any specific prerequisites or considerations

  2. WB2_REFERENCE_DOCUMENTS:

    • Purpose and typical use cases
    • Implementation examples
    • Any specific prerequisites or considerations

Any help or pointers to relevant resources would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Rebate to Payer capped on a Ship-to Customer
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement where the rebate is to be paid to the payer of the sales documents but the rebate paid needs to be capped per Ship-to party.

Example: Cap is 2000 $

Sales Orders for Ship-to party A, Payer C --> 3000 $ (Cap is reached, so 2000 $ needs to be paid)

Sales Orders for Ship-to party B ,Payer C---> 1500 $ (Cap is not reached , so 1500 $ needs to be paid)

Rebate needs to be paid to Payer C  --> Total Rebate is  2000 $ + 1500 $ = 3500 $

Is there any way that this is possible in standard.

Thanks,

Priya

entry.title: CCM create intercompany cost distribution / post vendor credit memo against SO billing docs q-tities
entry.content:
Hello,

I have two questions, posting them together as wondering if 1. can be used for the purpose of 2. or not: 

1. Is there somewhere to read about the CCM contract type "create intercompany cost distribution".

2. Is there a way to post a vendor credit memo against SO billing docs quantities, so the value of the vendor credit memo is spread over proportionally to these quantities. The result should be visible in FI, so FI can trace it to the sales, the value of the credit memo once posted should contribute to the gross margin.

Thank you!

entry.title: Settlement document - multiple tax codes
entry.content:
Hi Guys, 

I need help with some standard logic. We have an italien rebate document (credit memo) which has 2 tax conditions included (via external tool determined) - this all works perfectly fine. We have two tax conditions which determines correct tax codes wihtin the condition. 

But on WBRP-MWSK2 only the last determined is saved and only one tax code is transferd to accounting which is wrong! I´m not 100% sure if this would work as per standard or if our consulting company has played around to much in transfer manager and messed things up. Any helpful input on how it should work as per standard or what to look out for in transfer manager?! Any help highly appriciated!

entry.title: Customer Hierarchy In Condition Contract / delta accrual
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I am using a customer hierarchy node in a condition table for a condition contract. The accruals are generated via the normal SD F2 invoice and the customer hierarchy works fine. But when doing a delta accrual, the pricing analysis shows that the value for the customer hierarchy isnt found, while using the same condition table as in the SD invoice pricing. Any idea what the cause of this is.

entry.title: Possible link between SAP Transportation Management and Funds Management
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

We are currently working on the design of a full S4 implementation in the public sector. I'm focused on the transportation module implementation for a company where budgeting is key. We are facing a lot of challenges and requirements regarding the transportation cost budgeting.

Do you have any recommendations regarding the following points :

1. Standard settlement process would create a new service Purchase Order during the FSD posting. Currently the PO line doesn't have the correct assignment category as one freight order line could contain multiple orders with different account assignment.

- How can this detail be captured from TM to be available for correct controlling later on in the PO entries? 

- Can we use the cost distribution document to somehow transfer the correct account assignment of the reference document for each of the freight cost postings?

2. Is there anyway to extensively use the Freight Agreement Functionality in order to replace the creation of a PO to make provisions ( in order to have control on how much you are allowed to spend. )  based on your official contract with your Transporter?  I understand that the main functionality of the freight agreement and underlying master data is to automate charge calculation but here we need to create PO to have have an overview of the foreseen freight provisions.

3. Is it recommendable to post the freight cost of new freight order on an existing PO in S4?

Any guidance/advices regarding how those 2 modules can benefit from each other are welcome.

Thanks

entry.title: CCM - use multiple condition tables within one condition contract
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

Is it possible to use different rebate percentages across condition tables within the same condition contract? Without summing all of the percentages when settling the contract?

I have following business scenario:

  • Rebate: Agreement with customer to pay overall 6% rebate based on business volume. However, for one specific material group (let's call it material group A), the customer receives 8% rebate.
  • Accrual: Should follow the same logic as the rebate percentages i.e., 6% overall, but for material group A we want to accrue 8%.

I am trying to achieve this by using 2 condition tables within same access sequence:

  • Cond table 1: Condition contract / Material group
  • Cond table 2: Condition contract

I am entering both REA1 and RES1 in the condition contract:

  • Cond table 1: condition contract nr / material group A = 8%
  • Cond table 2: condition contract nr = 6%

Scenario: I only have business volume of sales of material within material group A.
Expectation: Both accruals and rebate would be posted with 8%
The issue I am encountering: At time of settlement both RES1 condition records are found, ending up in 14% instead of 8%. How can I set up my condition contract to achieve my goal? Business wants to avoid to enter all of the material groups, hence, I am trying to use multiple condition tables within one contract.

For the accruals, there is no issue, we can use the exclusive indicator in the access sequence together with routine 67.

Thank you.

entry.title: document type in Settlement Profile internal orders TKB1V-BLART
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

currently we need some further information to understanding the customized document type in the settlement profile of internal orders under S4.

- In which business transactions is this customized document type used? only for FI, only for CO transactions?

- What is the relation to the different ledgers?

It would be nice if you could share your experiences on this topic and how you use this field.

Thanks and best regards!

entry.title: Credit Memo amounts are not getting reduced in Delta Accruals in SAP Condition Contracts
entry.content: I have an issue in Condition Contract Settlement. Returns and Credit Memo amounts are not getting reduced in Delta Accruals. In WB2R_BUSVOL it shows the negative amount as part of the business volume. However, WB2R_BVDETAIL, it just makes the amount Zero. What is the reason behind this? How can I solve this?

entry.title: Request for Order Settlement Based on GL Account to Different WBS Elements
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire if it's possible to configure an order settlement rule that allows cost settlement based on General Ledger (GL) accounts to different WBS elements. Our business requirement is as follows:

  • Diesel costs (e.g., posted to a specific GL account) should be settled to WBS Element A (e.g., for fuel-related costs).

  • Spare parts costs (posted to another GL account) should be settled to WBS Element B (e.g., for material/repair costs).

In essence, we need to split the settlement of an order based on the cost origin (GL account) so that each type of cost is settled to the correct corresponding WBS element.

We request your guidance on whether this can be achieved via standard SAP functionality, such as:

  • Creating multiple settlement rules with account assignment filters (GL-based)?

  • Using source structures and assignment rules in settlement profiles?

  • Any enhancement/BAdI recommendations, if standard functionality does not support this?

Your assistance on how best to approach and configure this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Manual rebate process: Tax statement item missing for tax Code XA error when delta accrual and final
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

During manual settlement process using WB2R_DOC_Entry, i'm getting "Tax statement item missing for tax Code XA" error. This is happening only when there is delta accrual and final settlement dates on the same day 31.12.2024. After running delta accrual, the error is occurring while doing final settlement. The tax classification of customer and material is in place.

Delta accrual on 31st oct 2024 is happening but not final settlement on 31st Oct'24 as mentioned below screenshot. Please provide your inputs.

 

 

Settlement Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Final settlement is not considering the previous settlement amount in Multi Payee Condition contract
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have one issue in CCM- Condition contracts Management.

In Multi Payee Scenario, System is not adjusting the amount in the final settlement considering the partial settlement amount which was already settled but creating final settlement document with exact amount like in partial settlement document and not determining RES2 condition type.
Final settlement is considering previous settlement amount only when the Bill to party number is same as Sold to Party and determining RES2 in final settlement document.

Request you to share the inputs.

#Settlement Management   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales   SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Condition contracts - vendor rebates
entry.content: Hi, we are implementing CCM with vendor rebates. We need the final settlement to be posted to the GL account, not the vendor subledger (reconciliation) account. This is because we need to wait for the vendor invoice before posting it. Is this possible?

entry.title: Sales org specific number range for Settlement Document in Condition Contract Management CCM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to configure Sales org wise number ranges for Settlement Document in Condition Contracts Management. We have around 20 sales organization in the system, whenever the settlement document is created, the settlement document should be created with number range based on the Sales Organization. We do automatic settlement, manual settlement, Settlement reversal. Request to share the steps to do the configuration in the system.

Thank you in advance.

#Settlement Management   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales   SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Settlement management for paying commissions
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have the following commissions process today.

  1. Sales rep participates as a partner on sales orders
  2. Commission is calculated using a condition type in pricing procedure
  3.  Upon invoicing the sale, commission is transferred to Vistex using an incentive document and accrued
  4. Every two weeks the accrued documents are settled using a settlement calendar and commission aggregated and sent to an external payroll system.

I believe settlement management should be able to handle the above business scenario. We are planning to move to S4 RISE PCE and looking to simplify our landscape.

Thank you

entry.title: API for Settlement rule creation in WBS Element
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is there a standard API to create the settlement rule in WBS element by mass upload. 

Best Regards,

Channa

entry.title: Change Output determination in settlement document
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I have a question. I'm trying to print a settlement document using an output type created in NACE, but when I try to add the message, I get this screen:

But I would like this view to appear so I can add the output type that I already have with the SMARTFORM:

 

How can I change this view?

entry.title: Condition Contract Sample Request Payload For API
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I am working on API_CONDITION_CONTRACT - SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

I am looking for a sample request payload, which will contract Business Volume Selection Criteria, Settlement Calendar and Rebate Percentage.

Thanks,

Arindam Sur
